macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $77K, triggering major crypto market liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly dropped under $77,000, extending a multi-day selloff and helping trigger hundreds of millions of dollars in liquidations across the crypto market. Reports cite roughly $657 million to more than $660 million in liquidations over 24 hours amid rising volatility and ETF outflows.

items.title: Crypto market sees sharp liquidation spike as leverage unwinds
items.summary: Market-wide liquidations surged as leveraged positions were flushed out during the latest downturn, with some reports also citing over $85 million in liquidations in a 24-hour period. The move underscores how quickly sentiment has turned fragile across major assets.

items.title: Ethereum and XRP show relative strength while Bitcoin leads the downturn
items.summary: While Bitcoin weakened sharply, Ethereum and XRP have been described as holding key support zones better than BTC. Analysts say the market remains under pressure, but selective altcoin resilience suggests some traders are rotating into stronger assets.

items.title: CLARITY Act advances through Senate committee, marking a regulatory milestone
items.summary: CryptoSlate and CryptoPotato report that the CLARITY Act cleared a major Senate committee hurdle and is moving toward a full Senate vote. The bill is being watched closely for its potential impact on U.S. digital-asset market structure and stablecoin-related rules.

items.title: White House and banks clash over stablecoin rewards language in CLARITY Act discussions
items.summary: A new policy dispute emerged around stablecoin rewards provisions, with reports saying White House officials noted that some U.S. banks declined meetings aimed at resolving the issue. The disagreement highlights continuing friction between the banking sector and crypto policy makers.

items.title: Hacker steals more than $11 million from Verus-Ethereum bridge
items.summary: CryptoPotato reported a major exploit targeting the Verus-Ethereum bridge, with more than $11 million allegedly stolen. The incident adds to concerns about ongoing security risks in cross-chain infrastructure.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs post heavy outflows as prices weaken
items.summary: ETF flows remained a key bearish factor as spot Bitcoin and Ethereum products reportedly saw significant outflows during the latest price drop. Analysts say the combination of weak flows and leveraged liquidations is amplifying downside pressure.

items.title: BNB Chain publishes post-quantum cryptography migration research
items.summary: BNB Chain released a research report outlining a possible migration path for BSC toward post-quantum cryptography. The work signals early preparation for long-term blockchain security changes as quantum risks become a bigger industry focus.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: EY withdraws cybersecurity study after AI-generated errors discovered
items.summary: Ernst & Young reportedly pulled a cybersecurity report after researchers found fabricated data, incorrect citations, and references to non-existent reports. The incident has raised fresh concerns about AI hallucinations in client-facing consulting work.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Virtuos says it wants to bring GTA 5 and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Switch
items.summary: Port specialist Virtuos said it would like to port Grand Theft Auto V and Red Dead Redemption 2 to Nintendo Switch, following its previous work on the Switch version of L.A. Noire. The comments have fueled fresh speculation about Rockstar's back catalog coming to Nintendo hardware.

items.title: Terraria developer confirms cross-play is coming
items.summary: Re-Logic confirmed that Terraria cross-play is in development as the long-running sandbox game nears its 15th anniversary. The studio also teased special collector's items tied to the milestone.

items.title: Fortnite to preview The Mandalorian and Grogu ahead of film release
items.summary: Epic is giving Fortnite players a 10-minute sneak peek of The Mandalorian and Grogu before the movie hits theaters later this week. It is another example of Fortnite being used as a promotional platform for major entertainment launches.

items.title: Subnautica 2 sells 2 million in 12 hours
items.summary: VGC reported that Subnautica 2 had an explosive launch, reaching 2 million sales in its first 12 hours and peaking above 600,000 concurrent players. The strong debut came despite a troubled development cycle.

items.title: Two unannounced Xbox controllers reportedly surface
items.summary: According to VGC, two unreleased Xbox controllers appeared online, including what looks like an Elite Series 3 and a new cloud-focused pad. The leaks suggest Microsoft may be preparing new accessories for its gaming lineup.

items.title: Nintendo Switch Online adds more Virtual Boy games
items.summary: Nintendo has already added most of the Virtual Boy library to Switch Online with its latest update, bringing five new titles to subscribers. The move continues Nintendo's steady expansion of retro offerings on the service.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate email sparks GTA 6 pre-order speculation
items.summary: A Best Buy message to affiliates suggesting GTA 6 pre-orders begin next week has fueled new trailer and marketing speculation. The timing lines up with Take-Two's upcoming financial results, adding to fan anticipation.

items.title: MercurySteam lays off staff
items.summary: The Metroid Dread developer said it is dealing with layoffs, describing the situation as difficult and painful. The studio did not provide a full breakdown of how many employees were affected.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: TechNewsWorld highlights broader tech coverage, but recent items are mostly older or AI-focused
items.summary: The provided search results do not include clear non-AI technology headlines from the past 24 hours. Most visible items are dated in January 2026 or center on AI-related themes, so there is not enough current non-AI news to summarize reliably.
